solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int countBinarySubstrings(string s) {
        int count = 0;
        int prev = 0, curr = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i < s.size(); ++i) {
            if (s[i] == s[i - 1]) {
                ++curr;
            } else {
                prev = curr;
                curr = 1;
            }
            count += min(prev, curr);
        }
        return count;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through the string once, tracking the count of consecutive characters and comparing it with the previous group to determine valid substrings. It efficiently computes the result in linear time and constant space.
